data.title: Short sellers lower bets against S&P 500 financial stocks
data.description:
Average short interest across S&P 500 financial (NYSEARCA:XLF) stocks posted a moderate 4-basis-point decline to 1.78% of shares float in May, compared to 1.82% in the prior month. The prior month short interest has been revised from 1.77% amid inclusion of Coinbase Global (COIN) and exclusion of Discover Financial Services (DFS) in S&P 500 financial index (SP500-40). The number of shares shorted in May fell to 847.28M from 867.58M at the end of April.
 
The S&P 500 financial index (SP500-40) rose 4.29% in the last month, compared to 6.15% gain recorded by S&P 500.

Short interest, which could be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float).

Most shorted stocks:

Franklin Resources (BEN) - 9.33% vs 9.45% last month.

Apollo Global Management (APO) - 6.77% vs 6.92%.

 Coinbase Global (COIN) - 6.23% vs 4.92%.

Erie Indemnity Company (ERIE) - 6.15% vs. 5.85%.

 Regions Financial (RF) - 5.70% vs 5.71%.

Least shorted stocks:

Chubb (CB) – 0.74% vs 0.80% last month.

Mastercard (MA) – 0.75% vs 0.69% last month.

S&P Global (SPGI) – 0.78% vs 0.89.

JPMorgan (JPM) - 0.98% vs 1.01%.

The Charles Schwab (SCHW) - 1.00% vs 1.02%. 

 

Financial Services was at the top of the pack within the financial sector, with 2.10% short interest as of May end, up significantly from 2.01% as of April end.

Capital Markets were the second most shorted industry within the financial sector, with 2.07% short interest as of the end of May, a tick higher than 2.06% in April.

Insurance was the least shorted industry at 1.51% against 1.40% in the prior month.

data.title: Trump – Musk fallout continues as White House probes SpaceX deals: report
data.description:
The bitter public feud between Donald Trump and Elon Musk continues to simmer as the White House ordered the Defense Department and NASA to review billions of dollars' worth of contracts awarded to the Tesla (TSLA) CEO's SpaceX business, Reuters reported.

Citing four people familiar with the matter, the publication said that the Trump administration has called for the investigation to prepare for possible retaliatory action against Musk and his businesses.

The review is “for political ammunition,” one of the people said. Reuters previously reported that SpaceX is at risk of losing a key role it was expected to play in an ambitious new U.S. missile defense system called "Golden Dome" due to the Trump-Musk public spat.

It is not immediately clear if the White House  plans to terminate any of the roughly $22B in federal contracts awarded to SpaceX. SpaceX or Defense Department officials did not respond to requests for comments.

This week, Musk, who also owns neurotechnology firm Neuralink and generative artificial intelligence startup xAI, among others, expressed regret over some of the social media posts directed at Trump, noting, "They went too far."

data.title: Oman says Sunday's U.S.-Iran nuclear talks will not happen
data.description:
A sixth round of U.S. and Iran nuclear talks scheduled for Sunday will not happen, mediator Oman said on Saturday. 

The cancellation comes amid an escalating conflict between Israel and Iran.

"The Iran U.S. talks scheduled to be held in Muscat this Sunday will not now take place. But diplomacy and dialogue remain the only pathway to lasting peace," Oman's foreign minister Badr Albusaidi said on X (formerly Twitter).

The talks have been focused on curbing Iran's uranium enrichment activities and nuclear program in exchange for the easing of some economic sanctions imposed by the U.S.  

Israel late on Thursday hit Iran with a series of what it called "preemptive" strikes against nuclear sites and military targets. Iran responded with waves of missiles on Friday.

The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) has confirmed that Israeli airstrikes have hit Iran's Natanz and Esfahan nuclear sites.  

Oil prices (CL1:COM)(CO1:COM) have spiked in response to the conflict, U.S. stocks (SP500) have tumbled, and investors have flocked to traditional safe-haven asset gold (XAUUSD:CUR).  

Here are some exchange-traded funds of interest that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).  

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: IAEA says critical buildings at Iran's Esfahan nuclear site damaged
data.description:
The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) on Saturday said four critical buildings at Iran's Esfahan nuclear facility had been damaged by Israeli airstrikes, though no increase in radiation levels had been observed at the site.

The four buildings include the uranium conversion facility and the fuel plate fabrication plant, the IAEA said on X (formerly Twitter).

"Iran’s Nuclear Regulatory Authority has informed the IAEA that off-site radiation levels remain unchanged in Esfahan," the agency added.

The IAEA on Friday confirmed that Israeli airstrikes had also hit Iran's Natanz nuclear site. At that facility, the above-ground part of the pilot fuel enrichment plant, where Iran was producing uranium enriched up to 60% U-235, had been destroyed.              

Separately, Israel's military on Saturday said it had eliminated nine of Iran's senior nuclear scientists and experts, a claim that was refuted by the spokesperson of Iran's atomic organization.

Israel late on Thursday hit Iran with a series of what it called "preemptive" strikes against nuclear sites and military targets. Iran responded with waves of missiles on Friday.

Oil prices (CL1:COM)(CO1:COM) have spiked in response, U.S. stocks (SP500) have tumbled, and investors have flocked to traditional safe-haven asset gold (XAUUSD:CUR).  

Here are some Israel-focused exchange-traded funds of interest: (NYSEARCA:EIS), (BATS:IZRL), (NYSEARCA:ISRA), and (NYSEARCA:ITEQ).

And here are some ETFs that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).  

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: SpaceX launch of Axiom-4 space mission set for June 16
data.description:
Axiom Space’s latest commercial mission to the International Space Station aboard SpaceX’s (SPACE) Dragon spacecraft is now expected to take place on Thursday, NASA announced on Saturday.

Its crew, comprising astronauts from the U.S., India, Poland, and Hungary, was scheduled to lift off on June 11 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida aboard the SpaceX Dragon spacecraft on Falcon 9.

However, a new pressure anomaly detected in the ISS's Zvezda service module delayed the launch initially scheduled for May 29.

NASA’s fourth private astronaut mission to the ISS, Axiom-4, has faced multiple delays due to reasons such as unfavorable weather conditions and a liquid oxygen (Lox) leak discovered in SpaceX's (SPACE) Falcon-9 rocket.

“It is not uncommon for the agency and its international partners to adjust launches around changes in operations aboard the space station,” Axiom Space said.

The Axiom-4 crew, led by former NASA astronaut and director of human spaceflight at Axiom Space Peggy Whitson, will lift off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

data.title: Most consumer staple stocks see a decline in short bets in May
data.description:
The short interest on 14 out of 38 consumer staple stocks—part of the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLP)—increased in May vs. the previous month. The remaining 24 stocks saw a fall in their short interest.

Short bets fell for 11 out of 13 industries in the consumer staples sector. The average grand total short interest also fell to 2.05% in May from 2.17% in April for defensives.

Industry Analysis:

In the consumer defensive/staples sector, Distillers and Vintners remained the most shorted industry, with an average short interest of 7.59% vs. the previous month's 7.74%.

Brewers were the second most shorted industry had the second-highest short interest of 7.05% in May, vs. 6.48% last month.

 

Short interest in the Soft Drinks and Non-alcoholic Beverages segment was lowest at 1.14% vs. 1.31% prior. Household Products was the second-lowest segment, with a short interest of 1.19% vs. 1.20%.

Short interest, which could potentially be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company's float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float). To note, consumer staples often underperform in rising markets but beat the falls. 

Least shorted stocks:

  • Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM): 0.72% vs. 0.90% last month
  • Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO): 0.75% vs. 0.94%
  • Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG): 0.75% vs. 0.80%
  • Walmart (NYSE:WMT): 0.95% vs. 1.10%
  • Costco WHolesale (NASDAQ:COST): 1.23% vs. 1.36%

Most shorted stocks:

  • Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.B): 9.78% vs. 9.86%
  • Campbell Soup (NASDAQ:CPB): 9.72% vs. 9.38%
  • Molson Coors Beverage (NYSE:TAP): 7.05% vs. 6.47%
  • Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR): 6.51% vs. 7.38%
  • Hershey Foods (NYSE:HSY): 5.81% vs. 5.58%

The S&P 500’s consumer staples sector ETF (NYSEARCA:XLP) gained 2.11%, compared to broader S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) gain of 5.49%. 

ETFs to tab consumer staples: (NYSEARCA:VDC), (NYSEARCA:IYK), (NYSEARCA:FSTA), (NYSEARCA:KXI), (NYSEARCA:FXG), (NYSEARCA:RSPS).

data.title: Short interest across S&P 500 real estate stocks decrease in May
data.description:
Average short interest across S&P 500 real estate stocks declined to 2.55% of the shares float at May-end from 2.58% at the end of the prior month.

Short interest in a stock represents the number of shares sold short but not yet repurchased by the short seller. The metric is often expressed as a percentage of the shares outstanding.

The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) gained 0.75% in May, against the broader S&P 500 index's 5.49% increase.

The estimated weight of XLRE components in the S&P 500 index stood at 2.12% as of Friday, June 13.

Industry Analysis

Hotel & Resort REITs continued to be the most shorted subsector. Average short interest in May rose to 7.23% from 7.07% in April.

Office REITs followed, with bets against the subsector increasing to 6.24% from 6.02%.

Other Specialized REITs saw the largest jump in short interest among subsectors, while Real Estate Services logged the steepest fall.

Camden Property Trust (CPT) saw the biggest decline in short interest, with bets against the stock reducing by 63 basis points to 1.73% in May.

Notably, bets against VICI Properties (VICI) rose the most, to 3.13% from 2.57% a month ago.

Most shorted stocks

Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) - 7.23%, vs. 7.07% last monthBXP (BXP) - 6.24%, vs. 6.02% last monthMid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) - 4.06%, vs. 3.68% last monthCoStar Group (CSGP) - 3.84%, vs. 4.24% last monthRegency Centers (REG) - 3.49%, vs. 3.43% last month

Least shorted stocks

American Tower (AMT) - 1.11%, vs. 1.26% last monthCrown Castle (CCI) - 1.26%, vs. 1.35% last monthPrologis (PLD) - 1.27%, vs. 1.29% last monthCBRE Group (CBRE) - 1.39%, vs. 1.48% last monthInvitation Homes (INVH) - 1.51%, vs. 1.68% last month

data.title: Meta has reportedly eased cannabis search restrictions
data.description:
• Meta (NASDAQ:META) has seemingly rolled back a restriction that blocked searches for words like “marijuana” and “cannabis” on its social media platforms Facebook and Instagram, Marijuana Moment reported.
  • The Menlo Park, California-based tech giant revised its content moderation policies early this year, ending its third-party fact-checking program and relaxing its restrictions on hot-button topics such as immigration and gender identity.
  • However, the company continued to block marijuana-related search results and instead displayed a warning that encouraged users to report “the sale of drugs.”
  • According to Marijuana Moment, the policy appears to have come to an end, with search responses for content that was blocked early this year being generated without the warning as of Friday.
  • Meta (NASDAQ:META) and Facebook didn’t immediately respond to Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.

data.title: Insider trades: Nvidia, CrowdStrike among notable names this week
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) officials were seen selling the company’s shares for the second straight week. Other companies that witnessed insider activity this week include retail chains Costco (NASDAQ:COST) and Target (NYSE:TGT) and healthcare major CVS Health (NYSE:CVS). Below is a list of transactions that occurred between June 9 to June 13.

  • Nvidia's (NVDA) Director Tench Coxe sold 1M shares of the company at a price of $142.80 each, for a total value of $142.8M. Coxe retains ownership of 31.57M shares following the transaction. Meanwhile, another director at the firm, Mark Stevens, offloaded a total of 358,849 shares at a price range of $141.85 to $142.5, resulting in a total value of $51M. Following the transaction, he holds 36.47M shares in the company.
  • Michael Sentonas, president of CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) sold 25,000 shares of the company for $477 apiece, totaling about $11.93M. His holding in the company now stands at 422,204 shares.
  • Costco’s (COST) Executive Vice President Pierre Riel sold a stake worth $2M in the retail chain. He let go of 2,000 shares for about $1,014.15 each, reducing his shareholding to about 8,210 shares.
  • Matthew Liegel, the chief accounting officer of Target (TGT), sold 2,044 shares of common stock at a price of $100.9 per share, totaling $206,245. Following this transaction, Liegel owns 11,064 shares of Target’s common stock.
  • Patrick Conway, CEO of UnitedHealth Group’s (NYSE:UNH) subsidiary Optum, sold 589 shares of the parent company at $305 per share, totaling $179,645. After the sale, Conway directly holds 10,398 shares of UnitedHealth Group.
  • On the buy side, CVS Health’s (CVS) Director Guy Sansone purchased 1,570 shares for $63.7 each for a total value of $100,009. His holding has now increased to 12,007.36 shares in the firm.
  • Paul Middleton, CFO at Plug Power (NASDAQ:PLUG) revealed in a filing that he bought 650,000 shares of the company for about $1.03, investing a total of $672,035. Middleton now holds 2.61M shares in the company.

data.title: UK to launch new type of private stock market this year with watered-down disclosure rules
data.description:
The United Kingdom's financial regulator this week said it would be launching a new platform later this year on which shares in private companies can be traded. 

The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) said it had finalized the rules for the platform which will be called the Private Intermittent Securities and Capital Exchange System (PISCES).   

"The new platforms will give investors greater access and confidence to invest in exciting new companies, while early backers and employees can sell up and invest again," Simon Walls, executive director of markets at the FCA, said in a statement on Tuesday.

PISCES will allow the secondary trading of shares in private companies. The companies can set the floor and ceiling of their share prices, and will also have a say in who can buy the shares. 

"Access to PISCES will be limited to institutional investors, high-net-worth individuals, sophisticated investors and employees of participating companies. Investors will be provided with information about the risks involved to help them make informed decisions," the FCA said.

Notably, the proposed stock market requires significantly fewer disclosures from private companies. Responders to an FCA consultation on PISCES back in December last year generally supported the agency's approach for disclosure agreements.    

"However, many respondents considered our core disclosure proposals required too much information for this type of market. They argued we should streamline these proposals to avoid them being overly burdensome for private companies," the FCA's PISCES policy statement said.

"In response, we have streamlined core disclosure information requirements. Other respondents suggested we could clarify our disclosure proposals in some areas. Most respondents supported our flexible approach to additional company disclosure arrangements. We have therefore decided not to mandate a ‘sweeper’ model," the FCA added. 

As per the policy statement, the FCA has narrowed the core disclosure information required by a private company on the proposed stock market to just a description of any significant changes in the financial position of the company. 

"We are also removing disclosure on significant acquisitions or disposals and moving significant related party transactions into a separate standalone core disclosure information requirement as that it is more relevant for identifying potential conflicts of interest," the UK's financial regulator said. 

Additionally, private companies will not be required to reveal any litigation as a specific core disclosure information, and nor will they have to give out financial forecasts and business strategy. Moreover, PISCES listed companies will not be mandated to follow any specific accounting standards for financial information.

"PISCES will allow private companies to reach a broader range of investors, supporting investment in growth companies and boosting the competitiveness of UK markets," the FCA said.

Currently, the UK's main stock market is the FTSE 100 (UKX), which is a market-capitalization weighted index of UK-listed blue-chip companies. It is operated by FTSE Russell, a London Stock Exchange Group business.        

Here are some UK-linked exchange-traded funds of interest: (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NASDAQ:FKU), and (BATS:EWUS).
